title: bursera heliae
intro: Bursera heliae, also known as the 'Desert Ironwood', is a tree species endemic to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its highly valued and durable wood. Traditionally, the Desert Ironwood has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Bursera heliae are said to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant effects, potentially providing relief from conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The herbal preparations of Bursera heliae include tea, tincture, and salve, which can be used topically or internally to harness its potential health benefits.
